sourceMovie.title: End of Watch
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto End of Watch (2012) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shot Caller
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If End of Watch clicked for you, Shot Caller (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If End of Watch clicked for you, Street Kings (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Confessions of a Dangerous Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If End of Watch clicked for you, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after End of Watch?
faq.answer: Try Shot Caller, Street Kings, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind—each shares DNA with End of Watch while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is End of Watch?
faq.answer: End of Watch (2012) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is End of Watch worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
